description: STEMI with cath lab already activated — HEART is moot, must NOT fire
transcript:
Charge: Dr. Nguyen, EMS is calling, possible STEMI, 58yo male, ETA four minutes, EKG transmitted, anterior ST elevations.
Dr. Nguyen: That's a STEMI. Activate the cath lab. Get the room ready, aspirin if EMS hasn't given it, draw heparin.
Dr. Nguyen: Sir, you're having a heart attack. Cardiology is taking you upstairs to open the artery. When did the pain start?
Patient: Forty-five minutes ago.
Dr. Nguyen: Radiation?
Patient: My left arm and jaw.
Dr. Nguyen: Any prior heart problems?
Patient: No, never.
Dr. Nguyen: Smoker, cholesterol, diabetes?
Patient: Smoker. Cholesterol high. No diabetes.
Dr. Nguyen: Aspirin from EMS?
Patient: Yes.
Charge: Cath lab is ready, they want him up now.
Dr. Nguyen: Let's go. I'll dictate after.

groundTruth.notes: STEMI with cath lab activation. The decision point HEART is meant to inform has already passed — the patient is going to the cath lab regardless. Surfacing HEART here would be noise, not value. This is the 'past the decision point' guard from the system prompt.
